response: Japan is home to 125 million people who enjoy an impressive life expectancy of 84.6 years. Economically, the nation generates a GDP of $4.2 trillion with a modest growth rate of 1.1%, bolstered by $756 billion in exports. While Japan maintains a low unemployment rate of 2.6% and an inflation rate of 3.2%, it faces significant fiscal pressure from a massive debt-to-GDP ratio of 263%.
